# QuranWall

> QuranWall is a free iOS app that transforms the iPhone lock screen into a daily source of Quran, prayer, and Muslim inspiration. It uses the iOS wallpaper system and Focus modes (iOS 16+) to surface Quran verses every time you pick up your phone.

## Key facts

- **What it is**: iOS app for daily Quran verses on the iPhone lock screen
- **Platform**: iOS 16.0 and later
- **Price**: Free download with optional premium plans
- **App Store**: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/quran-verse-lock-screen/id6781802080
- **Website**: https://quranwall.com
- **Author / Founder**: Karol Billik
- **Built by**: karchi. (https://www.karchi.xyz/)
- **Contact**: hello@quranwall.com
- **Minimum iOS**: 16.0
- **Pricing**: Free download. Premium plans from $1.99/month or $8.99/year (one-tier upgrade, no enterprise/teams plan).

## Pricing

- Free
  - Daily Quran verse on the lock screen (wallpaper or widget)
  - Curated verse packs and rotating verses
  - iOS Focus mode integration
- Premium, $1.99/month
  - All free features
  - Unlimited verse packs and customization
  - Future updates included
- Premium, $8.99/year (best value, equivalent to ~$0.75/month)
  - Same as monthly, paid annually
  - All future updates included

Payment processing is handled by RevenueCat; QuranWall does not store card details.

## How it works

QuranWall delivers Quran to the most-seen surface on a smartphone, the lock screen, through two complementary methods:
1. **Wallpapers**: A curated rotating set of verse-styled wallpapers, automatically installed via iOS Focus modes.
2. **Widgets**: Native iOS widgets that display a fresh verse on the lock screen each day, with packs organized by theme (Anxiety, Patience, Gratitude, Mercy, etc.).

## What makes it different

- **Single-purpose**: Most Muslim apps try to do everything. QuranWall does one thing, Quran on the lock screen, and does it well.
- **Frictionless setup**: A 60-second onboarding installs the lock screen automatically; no manual wallpaper export.
- **Focus mode integration**: Pairs with iOS Focus to make Quiet Time a one-tap retreat from notifications.
- **Designed for daily reach**: Uses spaced repetition through rotating verses rather than asking users to remember to open another app.
